name: Diffract CLI Commands
description: Diffract is our branded UI/CLI layer on top of NemoClaw. 'diffract' is a symlink to 'nemoclaw' — all commands identical. Setup: diffract onboard, diffract list, diffract status. Sandbox: diffract <name> connect/status/logs/policy-add/policy-list/destroy. Credentials: diffract credentials list/reset <KEY>. Services: diffract start/stop. Other: diffract debug, diffract uninstall, diffract --version. Web UI project is in nemoclaw-ui/ (Next.js, package name 'diffract').

title: Diffract is branded layer over NemoClaw + OpenShell
rationale: Diffract is our product name. CLI is a symlink (diffract -> nemoclaw). Web UI (Next.js) in nemoclaw-ui/ replaces interactive CLI with single-form deploy + dashboard. Underlying NemoClaw env vars (NEMOCLAW_*) preserved since that's what the CLI reads internally. Enterprise target: SSO, RBAC, multi-tenant planned.

name: chat-ws-loopback-reorigination-hop
description: Chat /chat WebSocket on the dashboard (port 9119) requires a LOOPBACK client peer (web_server.py _ws_client_is_allowed, a DNS-rebinding defence). Behind Caddy a plain host socat forwards from the docker BRIDGE IP -> non-loopback peer -> WS upgrade 403 -> chat dead. An openshell/ssh -L tunnel does NOT fix it: the dashboard is docker-exec'd into the CONTAINER netns while the openshell ssh session lands in the sandbox WORKLOAD netns -> tunnel binds but 502s (no listener there). FIX (transport-only, no rebuild): in-container socat hop re-originates from the container's own 127.0.0.1 so the dashboard sees a loopback peer. Chain host 127.0.0.1:9119 -> CONTAINER_IP:9118 (bridge) -> 127.0.0.1:9119 (in container). Host listener bound to 127.0.0.1 (Caddy dials loopback). Lives in setup.sh sandbox-port-forwarder.sh with an end-to-end curl keepalive. Caveat: behind a reverse proxy the peer is always the proxy, so this neutralises the peer check in effect; real gates are the per-session token + Origin check. Verified 403->101 through Caddy + survives cold systemctl restart (commit ee41c4b).

Dashboard /chat shows 'Chat unavailable: the ptyprocess package is missing' even though the WS handshake is 101. The embedded TUI chat (--tui) spawns a PTY via hermes_cli/pty_bridge.py needing ptyprocess; PtyBridge.spawn() raises PtyUnavailableError and web_server.py:3517 sends the error as a TEXT FRAME over an already-established socket -> WS 101 does NOT prove chat works (never discriminated this bug). Root cause: NemoClaw/agents/hermes/Dockerfile.base HERMES_UV_EXTRAS defaulted to 'messaging web' (no pty), so uv sync --frozen never installed the [pty] extra (uv.lock pins ptyprocess==0.7.0 under extra==pty). CHAT endpoint is /api/pty (NOT /api/ws).
resolution: (1) Dockerfile.base HERMES_UV_EXTRAS='messaging web pty' (root cause, baked on base rebuild, covers fresh client deploys); (2) setup.sh sandbox-port-forwarder.sh presence-checked idempotent 'pip install ptyprocess==0.7.0' before the --tui launch (self-heals containers recreated by redeploys without a rebuild; no-op once image carries pty). Verify by reading the post-101 frame on /api/pty: working chat streams terminal output (Diffract TUI banner), broken sends 'Chat unavailable'. Commit 41909ca.

name: dashboard-hermes-rebrand-safe-sweep
description: Removing visible Hermes branding from the dashboards. SAFE RULE: replace the standalone capital-H brand word via perl s/\bHermes\b/Diffract/g ONLY on pure-UI-text files: hermes/web/index.html (the title tag), hermes/web/src/i18n/*.ts (16 locales; the word boundary spares camelCase KEYS like updateHermes/updatingHermes so only string VALUES change), hermes/web/src/themes/presets.ts (Hermes Teal). Plus targeted edits for the session-token error strings in hermes/web/src/lib/api.ts and gatewayClient.ts, and diffractui Dashboard.tsx (Hermes Web Dashboard). MUST NOT touch (a blanket sed corrupts these contracts): the X-Hermes-Session-Token HTTP header (api.ts:31), window.__HERMES_* globals, HERMES_BASE_PATH, the /api/hermes/ routes, the HERMES_QWEN_ env prefix. Left truthful because they are real things (do not fake): the nemohermes CLI command (diffractui Dashboard.tsx:301), the ~/.hermes config paths plus hermes CLI command refs shown in the Env/Config/Plugins/Skills screens, and code/CSS comments. tsc and npm run build catch any accidental key rename. DEPLOY CHAINS: agent dashboard (hermes/web) builds to hermes_cli/web_dist; the LIVE source of truth is the HOST agent dir /usr/local/lib/hermes-agent (the sandbox-port-forwarder docker-cps its web_dist into the container on every restart), NOT the repo - so rebuild THERE then docker cp into the container /opt/hermes/web_dist. diffractui runs next start from the repo at /root/diffract-main/diffractui - git pull, npm run build (.next), systemctl restart diffractui. GOTCHA fixed: setup.sh _overlay_host copied web/src but not web/index.html, which is why the browser title survived the earlier rebrand. Verify by grepping the BUILT bundle / .next AND the live served /agent/ title, not just that it built. Commits 12a1cf9 (source) and the live redeploy.

title: Genericize ~/.hermes paths/command refs in dashboard UI (not fake, not leave)
rationale: User wanted NO visible hermes in the dashboard but the ~/.hermes config paths and hermes/nemohermes command refs are real. Faking them as ~/.diffract would show paths that do not exist on the box; leaving them keeps hermes visible. Chosen middle path: genericize - strip the ~/.hermes/ prefix so paths read config.yaml / plugins/ / skills/ (truthful, just relative), remove the parenthetical CLI-parity notes, and reword English (Remove this plugin?, No skills found.). Applied across all 17 locales incl full-width Chinese parens + a Hungarian declension. Left ONLY invisible contracts (X-Hermes-Session-Token, __HERMES_* globals, /api/hermes routes, HERMES_QWEN_, hermes_home field), the nemohermes CLI command, and code comments. STILL OPEN: HERMES_DOCS_URL=https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/ (the Docs tab) is an external site, not a label - flagged to user. Commits 12a1cf9 + b38d9c5. See [[dashboard-hermes-rebrand-safe-sweep]].

name: agent-self-identity-soul-md
description: The chat agents self-identity (what it says when greeting, e.g. I am Hermes Agent running in the NemoClaw sandbox) is driven by SOUL.md at HERMES_HOME (= /sandbox/.hermes/SOUL.md in the container). SOUL.md is slot #1 of the system prompt (agent/system_prompt.py via load_soul_md in agent/prompt_builder.py) and OVERRIDES DEFAULT_AGENT_IDENTITY. The deployment SOUL.md is baked by NemoClaw/agents/hermes/Dockerfile (was an inline printf with Hermes/NemoClaw/OpenShell text). A SECOND, code-level source of the word Hermes Agent is HERMES_AGENT_HELP_GUIDANCE (prompt_builder.py), always appended in system_prompt.py. KEY FINDING (verified with hermes -z one-shot): a strong positive slot-#1 SOUL.md dominates BOTH the help-guidance Hermes mention AND the models inference of NemoClaw/OpenShell from env vars (NEMOCLAW_*, OPENSHELL_*) - so you do NOT need to edit upstream agent Python. FIX: edit SOUL.md only (data, safe to apply live; NEVER live-patch the agent Python/TUI - version skew ends the session). Use a positive identity (advisor: do not enumerate forbidden brands - that primes them) plus a generic do-not-describe-the-platform line. Single source of truth: NemoClaw/agents/hermes/SOUL.md, COPYd by the Dockerfile (fresh deploys) and re-applied live by setup.sh (host stage + self-healing forwarder step that overwrites an old-marker SOUL on each container start, idempotent/self-deactivating). VERIFY with hermes -z greeting + an adversarial who/where probe, not by cat-ing the file. Commit 1d2af4d. Related [[dashboard-hermes-rebrand-safe-sweep]].

name: dashboard-chat-via-gateway-v1-api
description: The agent dashboard /agent Chat tab can be a normal message-bubble chat (not the xterm TUI). The dashboard backend (web_server.py) only exposes /api/pty (the terminal), but the agent also runs an OpenAI-compatible gateway on :8642 that Caddy proxies at origin /v1/* (handle /v1/* reverse_proxy 127.0.0.1:8642). That gateway runs the FULL agent (tools/skills/SOUL; ~14.5k prompt tokens) with NO auth (API_SERVER_KEY unset, /v1/models returns 200) and standard OpenAI SSE streaming (data: {choices:[{delta:{content}}]}), model id hermes-agent. So a chat UI is FRONTEND-ONLY: rewrite hermes/web/src/pages/ChatPage.tsx to POST /v1/chat/completions (leading slash = origin root, NOT under the /agent base path) with stream:true and render bubbles; keep the { isActive } prop so App.tsx persistent chat host is unchanged. Deploy like any web change (rebuild web_dist on the host agent dir + docker cp into the container) - no agent image rebuild. The Chat tab visibility is gated by __HERMES_DASHBOARD_EMBEDDED_CHAT__ (set true by hermes dashboard --tui), which stays true. Removing the TUI also removes its Hermes Agent welcome banner. Commit on this work.

The chat UI backend (agent OpenAI-compatible gateway, Caddy /v1/* -> 127.0.0.1:8642) returns 502 on fresh sandboxes because the gateway runs in the sandbox WORKLOAD netns, not the container bridge netns. The gateway is started by nemoclaw-start over the openshell ssh session; its api_server listens on 127.0.0.1:18642 in the workload netns, fronted by an in-sandbox socat on :8642 (also workload netns). docker exec lands in the CONTAINER (bridge) netns and CANNOT see 8642/18642 (ss shows nothing, curl 000) - that is the diagnostic tell. setup.sh port-forwarder used socat TCP-LISTEN:8642 -> CONTAINER_IP:8642 (bridge), which cannot reach the workload netns -> 502. It worked only when onboards own openshell forward won the :8642 bind race over the socat (flaky). This is the SAME netns split as the 9119 chat-WS issue, opposite direction (9119 dashboard = container netns -> in-container loopback hop; 8642 gateway = workload netns -> ssh -L tunnel).
resolution: Replace the 8642 socat in setup.sh sandbox-port-forwarder.sh with an openshell loopback forward (openshell forward start --background 8642 SANDBOX, an ssh -L that lands in the workload netns) + a keepalive (re-establish if the 127.0.0.1:8642 listener drops). Live unblock: kill the 8642 socat, openshell forward start --background 8642 hermes, then curl 127.0.0.1:8642/health = 200. Verified durable across a forwarder restart. Commit on this work.

description: After fixing the 8642 502, the chat UI still failed with 403. Cause: the agent gateways api_server rejects requests that carry a browser Origin (and/or Referer) header as a DNS-rebinding/CSRF defence, and its allowed-origins list does not include the public host. Diagnostic tell: server-side curl to /v1/chat/completions with NO Origin returns 200, but the same request WITH -H Origin: https://<host> returns 403 (response header server: aiohttp = the gateway, not Caddy). Browsers always send Origin on JSON POSTs, so the same-origin chat UI is always 403d.
resolution: Strip Origin (and Referer) on Caddys /v1/* reverse_proxy: add header_up -Origin and header_up -Referer inside the handle /v1/* reverse_proxy block (both the IP and domain CADDY_CONFIG variants in setup.sh). Caddy is a trusted proxy fronting the gateway; same-origin is the only legit client; cross-origin reads stay blocked by the browser CORS (gateway sends no ACAO). Live: edit /etc/caddy/Caddyfile + systemctl reload caddy; verify POST /v1 with an Origin header is 200. SECURITY NOTE: /v1 is unauthenticated, so this removes the browser-origin guard for it - fine for the same-origin dashboard behind the admin gate, revisit if /v1 is exposed more broadly. Related [[gateway-8642-workload-netns-socat-502]] and [[dashboard-chat-via-gateway-v1-api]].

title: Persist dashboard chat conversations in localStorage, not server sessions
rationale: User wanted chat history to survive reload and to revisit old conversations. The agent gateway /v1/chat/completions is STATELESS (no session id returned to continue a conversation), and the agents own sessions (/api/sessions, source=api_server) live inside the sandbox and are DESTROYED whenever the sandbox is recreated - which this user does frequently. So server sessions cannot reliably back the chat history. Chose client-side localStorage (key diffract.chat.v1: conversations[] + activeId) in hermes/web ChatPage.tsx: a conversation sidebar (new/select/delete) + persist on every change. Survives reloads AND sandbox recreations (browser-side). History is resent to /v1 each turn (OpenAI-style). Tradeoffs: per-browser (not cross-device), and continuing an old chat after a recreate is answered from the visible transcript only (fresh agent, no server memory). If cross-device/server-backed history is needed later, use the OpenAI Responses API (/v1/responses exists, stateful) or /api/sessions, accepting they vanish on sandbox destroy. Frontend-only, no backend/image change. See [[dashboard-chat-via-gateway-v1-api]].

name: diffractui-files-api
description: Files tab API (diffractui/src/app/api/files/route.ts) lets an admin browse/upload/download/delete files in the agent sandbox. Root-on-host gated only by admin session, so: re-verify session per method (requireSession, defense-in-depth on top of proxy.ts), use execFile/spawn with argv arrays and NO shell, confine every path to /sandbox with path.posix.normalize + prefix check (rejects ..), block writes/deletes to /sandbox/.hermes and deletion of /sandbox root, cap uploads at 100MB checking Content-Length before buffering. Upload flow: host temp file then docker cp then docker exec chown sandbox:sandbox + chmod 0644 (docker cp lands as root). CRITICAL: resolve sandbox name to container via the exact docker label openshell.ai/sandbox-name=NAME (plus openshell.ai/managed-by=openshell), NOT by container name. OpenShell names containers openshell-NAME-UUID, so a name=^openshell-NAME$ filter 404s for the real nemoclaw name like hermes. The UI passes the nemoclaw sandbox name (e.g. hermes); status route hides this with a detectSandbox fallback, files API resolves by label. Verified live: agent read an uploaded file via /v1.

title: Persist sandbox CLI tools by baking them into the hermes image
rationale: OpenShell sandboxes have no volume/bind-mount (by design); /sandbox is the container writable layer and is destroyed on nemoclaw destroy. nemoclaw onboard REBUILDS the sandbox image on each create from agents/hermes/Dockerfile (confirmed: onboard resolves AGENTS_DIR = ROOT/agents where ROOT = resolve(dist/lib,..,..) = the installed CLI repo root; the bundled ~/.nemoclaw/source is NOT used at runtime). So the durable way to persist a tool is to add a build layer to that Dockerfile. GHL CLI is baked via git clone of public hrubee/GHL-CLI then npm ci + npm run build then symlink ghl into /usr/local/bin (on PATH). node/npm/git are in the base image hermes-sandbox-base and build-time network works. node_modules retained (dist/index.js needs it). Takes effect on next sandbox recreate only. Working-file (user data) persistence is a SEPARATE problem: no volumes means it must be host-backed (restore-on-boot + backup-on-destroy); never restore /sandbox/.hermes (rebuilt at image build + guarded by /etc/nemoclaw/hermes.config-hash trust anchor in nemoclaw-start.sh).

name: sandbox-file-persistence
description: Persist a sandbox user working files across destroy/recreate via host-backed tar (no volumes exist). scripts/diffract-persist.sh (installed to /usr/local/bin by setup.sh) has backup/restore/backup-if-ready subcommands; resolves the container by openshell.ai/sandbox-name label; tars /sandbox to /var/lib/diffract/persist/NAME/home.tar.gz excluding .hermes .nemoclaw .cache .npm node_modules GHL-CLI. Safety: restore runs once per container lifetime gated by in-container marker /sandbox/.diffract-restored (never clobbers a running sandbox newer files); periodic backup is gated on the marker so an empty fresh sandbox cannot overwrite good host data; backups atomic via tmp+mv and never overwrite a good archive with an empty/failed one; never touches .hermes. Wired in: setup.sh forwarder does restore-on-boot + backup-if-ready every ~5min in keepalive; diffractui deploy DELETE handler does best-effort backup-before-destroy. The live forwarder is kept in sync with setup.sh by extracting its heredoc with awk. Verified live without destroy: backup excludes correct, restore preserves sandbox:sandbox ownership, marker gating works.

OpenShell confines the sandboxed agent workload filesystem READS to an allowlist: it can read its home /sandbox and runtime /opt/hermes, but NOT arbitrary image paths. A CLI baked into /opt/diffract-tools (world-readable -rwxr-xr-x, traversable parents) is still EACCES when the agent (uid 998 sandbox) tries to open it via openshell sandbox exec, even though docker exec into the outer container reads it fine. The agent workload is a separate containerd-overlay context from docker exec; /sandbox is shared+writable (files written via docker exec are visible to the workload), /opt and /usr/local/bin come from the image. Diagnosis: openshell sandbox exec --name hermes -- sh -lc reading /opt/hermes = OK, /opt/diffract-tools = FAIL. Fix shipped: universal installer install-diffract-tools.sh bakes tools into /sandbox/.diffract-tools/<name> + symlinks /usr/local/bin/<bin>; registry diffract-tools.json. NOTE: openshell sandbox exec rejects newline chars in the command arg (put commands on one line).
resolution: Install agent-facing tools under /sandbox (agent home), not arbitrary paths like /opt; symlink the entrypoint onto PATH at /usr/local/bin. Verified via openshell sandbox exec.

name: universal-tool-connection
description: Universal secure mechanism to connect a third-party CLI to the sandboxed agent so it can call the tool API without holding the secret. Validated end-to-end with GHL CLI (agent listed real GoHighLevel contacts using only placeholders). Three layers, registry-driven via NemoClaw/agents/hermes/diffract-tools.json: (1) INSTALL - install-diffract-tools.sh bakes each tool into /sandbox/.diffract-tools/NAME (MUST be /sandbox; OpenShell confines agent reads) + symlinks /usr/local/bin/BIN; optional patch step for proxy-compat fixups. (2) CREDENTIALS - register an OpenShell generic provider holding secretEnv + non-secret configEnv; sandbox sees only placeholders openshell:resolve:env:..., the L7 proxy substitutes real values at egress in BOTH headers and query params. detach+re-attach is REQUIRED to inject newly-added credentials into a running sandbox. (3) EGRESS - policy update SANDBOX --add-endpoint HOST:443:full --binary BIN --wait per apiHost. CRITICAL: on OpenShell 0.0.39 egress is denied unless the peer binary is named explicitly (--binary /usr/local/bin/node for node tools, /usr/bin/curl); empty binaries fail-closed. PROXY-COMPAT: the sandbox routes egress through an MITM proxy (NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS=/etc/openshell-tls/openshell-ca.pem); node undici/fetch and curl work, but axios legacy adapter aborts (stream has been aborted) - fix axios tools with adapter:fetch. scripts/diffract-tool-connect.sh automates layers 2-3 from the registry (secret read from env, never argv).

title: tool-connection-security-scoping
rationale: Honest go-to-market boundary: we proved the agent cannot leak a tool secret, but we did NOT prove it can only reach the allowlisted host, so the two claims must be sold separately to avoid overstating the boundary.

name: universal-installer-clean-build-verified
description: The universal tool-installer path is verified by a clean from-scratch docker build, not just inferred. On the VPS, docker build -f NemoClaw/agents/hermes/Dockerfile --build-arg BASE_IMAGE=ghcr.io/nvidia/nemoclaw/hermes-sandbox-base:latest -t diffract-tools-verify:test . completed end-to-end (image 164e3ea9, 3.81GB). Build step 11/32 ran install-diffract-tools.sh, which cloned hrubee/GHL-CLI, applied the axios adapter:fetch proxy-compat patch, ran npm ci plus npm run build, and symlinked /usr/local/bin/ghl. Smoke test: docker run --rm --entrypoint /usr/local/bin/node diffract-tools-verify:test /usr/local/bin/ghl --version prints 0.1.0 (rc 0). This closes the previously-unproven fresh-recreate gap: a brand-new sandbox image carries the baked tool on PATH without any manual /sandbox placement. Adding a tool is one registry entry in NemoClaw/agents/hermes/diffract-tools.json - no Dockerfile edit. Note: build emits two harmless SecretsUsedInArgOrEnv warnings for NEMOCLAW_PROVIDER_KEY (pre-existing, unrelated to the tool installer).

name: tool-advertise-skill-discovery
description: ADVERTISE is the 4th layer of universal-tool-connection - it makes the sandboxed hermes agent AWARE of a baked CLI. Install plus connect plus egress make a tool runnable and credentialed but do NOT make the agent discover it: hermes discovers capabilities from its skill catalog and nothing put the tool there, so a fresh session would not know ghl exists (it only ran before because it was told to). MECHANISM verified on the live box: hermes auto-discovers any SKILL.md dropped under /sandbox/.hermes/skills - hermes skills list shows it enabled with NO .bundled_manifest edit; the curator (hermes curator run) is only an optional usage-based optimizer, not a discovery gate (dry-run reported 0 candidates, skill already enabled). IMPLEMENTATION: advertise-diffract-tools.sh reads diffract-tools.json and emits one SKILL.md per tool (with an optional skill block: name/title/summary/tags/examples) into /sandbox/.hermes/skills/diffract-tools/NAME. It runs in the Dockerfile AFTER the .hermes setup (skills dir and SOUL.md only exist late; skills are NOT part of the config-integrity hash so it is safe after the hash pin). The generated skill body tells the agent the tool is PRE-AUTHENTICATED - run it directly, the env value is an openshell:resolve placeholder by design, never ask the user for a key (which would defeat confidentiality). SOUL.md also gets a generic always-in-context line; it is generic (not per-tool) so it survives the live setup.sh SOUL forwarder re-apply. PROVEN by clean docker build: build step 33/34 ran advertise, and a fresh container from the image lists ghl-crm enabled and has the baked SKILL.md and SOUL line. Adding a tool's discovery = a skill block in the registry; no code edit.

name: how-agent-discovers-tools-remote-llm
description: How the sandboxed agent KNOWS it has a baked tool, given the LLM runs remotely (not in the sandbox). The agent has two halves: the BRAIN (LLM) runs remotely on NVIDIA inference and never touches the sandbox filesystem; the HARNESS (hermes runtime) runs INSIDE the sandbox, holds the files (SOUL.md, skills) and executes tool calls. The bridge is system-prompt assembly done by the harness: AIAgent._build_system_prompt in /opt/hermes/agent/prompt_builder.py calls build_skills_system_prompt, which scans every ENABLED, platform-compatible SKILL.md (skipping names in the disabled set), takes each frontmatter name + description, and compiles a compact skill index. SOUL.md identity plus that skill index are injected into the system prompt sent to the remote model on every session. So the model knows a tool exists because the in-sandbox harness narrates it in the prompt each turn; the model never reads SKILL.md directly. The full SKILL.md body is pulled on demand (progressive disclosure) when the model engages the skill, keeping the always-present catalog compact. PROVEN deterministically on the live box: invoking build_skills_system_prompt (after clear_skills_system_prompt_cache) produced a 10681-char prompt containing the line - ghl-crm: List and manage GoHighLevel CRM data ... so the catalog the remote model receives includes our skill, not just the disk file. CACHE GOTCHA: the skill index has a two-layer cache - an in-process LRU per running session and a disk snapshot validated by mtime/size. Writing a SKILL.md by raw file write does NOT clear a running session in-process cache, so an already-open session will not see the new skill until it reloads; a NEW session or a fresh recreate from the baked image builds the prompt fresh and sees it. The clean way to add a skill to a live session is the hermes reload_skills command or the hermes skills CLI (which clears the cache), not a raw file write.

name: diffract-agent-autonomous-tool-use-proven
description: FULL end-to-end proof that the in-sandbox Diffract agent autonomously discovers AND uses a baked tool. Driven via hermes one-shot mode through the workload context: openshell sandbox exec --name hermes -- hermes -z PROMPT (using openshell sandbox exec, NOT docker exec, so the OpenShell egress proxy and credential injection apply; docker exec would lack them). The prompt was plain English (Look up my CRM contacts and summarize) and DID NOT name ghl. The agent on its own: (1) discovered the ghl-crm skill from the catalog the harness compiled into its system prompt, (2) chose to run ghl contacts list itself, (3) read real GoHighLevel data via the placeholder-to-egress-injection path (token never in sandbox), (4) answered in natural language (25 contacts, names, offered next steps). The live agent stack inside the sandbox: hermes gateway run (the always-on agent the dashboard talks to) plus hermes dashboard --host 0.0.0.0 --skip-build --insecure --tui (the Diffract UI chat backend). hermes -z is the clean headless way to drive the real agent loop for demos/tests and capture only the final answer. This confirms the discovery mechanism end-to-end through the real agent, not just by inspecting the built prompt. Security caveat unchanged: confidentiality proven; data-exfil via the raw-socket bypass is still gated on the OpenShell 0.0.57 upgrade.

name: tool-survives-sandbox-recreate-proven
description: Confirmed by a REAL destroy/recreate on the live VPS (not just a clean build) that the universal tool stack survives. After the user destroyed and recreated the hermes sandbox: (1) the ghl binary came back baked - /usr/local/bin/ghl -> /sandbox/.diffract-tools/ghl/dist/index.js with a fresh build timestamp, proving nemoclaw onboard rebuilds the image from the updated agents/hermes/Dockerfile (install + advertise layers); (2) the ghl-crm skill was enabled in hermes skills list and the SOUL.md pre-authenticated line was present; (3) the global OpenShell provider ghl survived with its 2 credential keys (token + location) intact - providers are global/host-side, NOT sandbox state. The ONLY thing wiped is the per-sandbox connection: openshell sandbox provider list hermes showed No providers attached, and the egress policy reset. Re-connect needs NO token because the provider still holds it - just: openshell sandbox provider detach hermes ghl (no-op) then attach, then openshell policy update hermes --add-endpoint services.leadconnectorhq.com:443:full --rule-name ghl-api --binary /usr/local/bin/node --wait. After that, env in the sandbox showed GHL_PRIVATE_TOKEN and GHL_LOCATION_ID placeholders present, and the real Diffract agent (hermes -z one-shot, prompt did NOT name ghl) autonomously discovered the skill, ran ghl contacts list, and returned 25 real contacts with names. IMPROVEMENT CANDIDATE: auto-reconnect on boot (re-attach providers + re-apply tool egress policy from a host-side manifest) would make the connection survive too, closing the original tools-must-not-vanish ask fully.

On OpenShell 0.0.39 the long-running hermes gateway daemon (what the Diffract dashboard chat talks to) is NOT in the credential-injection egress path, so the dashboard agent CANNOT use proxy-injected tools like ghl. Proven by reading /proc/PID/environ of the running gateway: it has NO HTTPS_PROXY, NO NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS (the MITM CA), and NO openshell:resolve placeholders - only sandbox metadata (OPENSHELL_ENDPOINT, OPENSHELL_SANDBOX_ID). By contrast an openshell sandbox exec session env DOES have all of them (HTTP(S)_PROXY=http://10.200.0.1:3128, NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS=/etc/openshell-tls/..., OPENSHELL_SANDBOX=1, the GHL placeholders) and ghl resolves there (25 real contacts). Driving the running gateway via its :8642 OpenAI-compatible endpoint, the agent ran ghl and the gateway log showed Tool terminal returned error No Private Integration Token; nsenter -a into the gateway namespace with the placeholder gave 401 Invalid JWT (literal placeholder sent, not substituted). Inference still works on the gateway only because it uses a SEPARATE special-cased channel (OPENSHELL_ENDPOINT direct to the OpenShell host), NOT the substitution proxy - so inference working does NOT imply general tool egress works. docker restart does NOT help: OpenShell populates the container/gateway env with provider placeholders only at sandbox-create from providers attached THEN; nemoclaw-start refresh_hermes_provider_placeholders copies only placeholders already in the entrypoint env into .env and re-pins the config hash. The exec path (openshell sandbox exec -- hermes -z) works today. Earlier claim that the dashboard hits the same agent so tools work there too was WRONG.
resolution: Upgrade OpenShell 0.0.39 to 0.0.57 (workload in an isolated netns whose only egress is the substitution proxy, so the long-running gateway also routes through it) - same upgrade that closes the raw-socket exfil gap (mx-d0d580/mx-17f1e6). Until then, drive agent tool-use via the exec path: openshell sandbox exec -- hermes -z. An auto-reconnect-on-boot hook cannot fix the dashboard path because injection is exec-session-scoped.

name: openshell-0057-fixes-daemon-credential-injection
description: PROVEN in an isolated docker:dind lab (own PID namespace + own dockerd; live demo never touched) that OpenShell 0.0.57 fixes the gateway-daemon credential-injection failure that 0.0.39 has (see gateway-daemon-not-in-credential-injection-path). Setup: ran the real 0.0.57 openshell-gateway in the lab, created a sandbox from a minimal debian-slim image (curl, iproute2, iptables, nftables) with a generic provider (TEST_TOKEN=ZZSECRETVALUE99, TEST_ID) attached AT CREATE via openshell sandbox create --name t2 --from <dockerfile-dir> --provider testprov --policy <file>. Two results: (1) DELIVERY - reading /proc/PID/environ of every sandbox process showed the long-running workload daemon (sleep infinity, the gateway analog) HAD the openshell:resolve:env:vNNN_TEST_TOKEN placeholder in its env, exactly like an exec session; the supervisor (pid 1, openshell-sandbox) correctly had none. On 0.0.39 the long-running hermes gateway had NO placeholder at all - that was the root cause. (2) RESOLUTION - from the sandbox, curl https://postman-echo.com/headers with header Authorization: Bearer <placeholder> came back echoed as authorization: Bearer ZZSECRETVALUE99, i.e. the proxy substituted placeholder->real secret at egress. So 0.0.57 both delivers the placeholder to a daemon AND resolves it at the proxy. KEY GOTCHAS for the lab: openshell-gateway is glibc-dynamic so the lab needs a glibc base (cruizba/ubuntu-dind, run with a keep-alive CMD like sleep infinity + a /var/lib/docker volume, else it exits code 0 after dockerd starts); the static openshell CLI runs anywhere; --from must be a Dockerfile DIRECTORY to build locally (a bare image tag triggers a registry pull -> denied); sandbox name conflicts need openshell sandbox delete (not destroy). Final confirmation with the actual hermes gateway image happens at Phase 2 of the upgrade plan. This clears the Phase-0 gate: the 0.0.57 upgrade WILL make the dashboard agent able to use tools.

name: diffractui-tools-tab-connect-ui
description: The Diffract dashboard has a Tools tab (diffractui/src/app/components/ToolsTab.tsx + api/tools/route.ts) giving point-and-click UX over the universal tool-connection registry. GET /api/tools?sandbox=NAME reads NemoClaw/agents/hermes/diffract-tools.json and computes LIVE status per tool across four layers: installed (docker exec test -e /usr/local/bin/BIN in the sandbox container resolved by openshell.ai/sandbox-name label), advertised (test -e /sandbox/.hermes/skills/diffract-tools/SKILLNAME/SKILL.md), connected (provider name appears in openshell sandbox provider list SANDBOX), egress (the tool rule-name TOOL-api or an apiHost appears in openshell policy get SANDBOX --full). POST /api/tools runs diffract-tool-connect.sh SANDBOX TOOL REGISTRY to attach the provider + apply egress policy. SECURITY: same model as files/route.ts - re-verify admin session every method, execFile argv arrays no shell, validate sandbox name regex + tool exists in registry + credential keys exactly match the tool secretEnv+configEnv. The secret is taken from the POST body and passed to the connect script ONLY via the child env (PATH widened with node dir + /usr/local/bin), never argv, never logged, never persisted, never returned. Registry + connect-script paths resolve via env override then repo-relative (process.cwd is the diffractui dir) then /usr/local/share/diffract and /usr/local/bin (setup.sh installs both). MVP scope is connect+status (no rebuild); adding NEW tools/triggering a rebuild is a later phase. GOTCHA: FilesTab and ToolsTab both trip the Next16 react-hooks rule Calling setState synchronously within an effect (error-level in lint) but next build still compiles successfully - the build does not gate on it; match the existing FilesTab pattern rather than contorting around it. Typechecks + next build pass.

name: diffractui-add-tool-live-install
description: The diffractui Tools tab can ADD a new CLI from the UI (Phase 2). Flow: POST /api/tools/add validates the form (TOOL_NAME a-z0-9-, repo https...git, entry path, host:port, UPPER_SNAKE keys, build/patch single-line - build runs in-sandbox by design, admin-gated), appends the entry to diffract-tools.json (atomic tmp+rename, dup-checked), then spawns scripts/diffract-tool-add.sh DETACHED with stdio to /tmp/diffract-add-NAME.log and returns {job:name} immediately. GET /api/tools/add?job=NAME polls that log for the trailing ===DONE rc=N=== marker (running/done/failed). ToolsTab shows a + Add tool form (node-tool defaults) and an install-progress panel that polls every 3s and refreshes status on completion. KEY CORRECTNESS POINT: a live add runs install/advertise INSIDE the already-running sandbox whose BAKED scripts predate the single-tool --only arg (added as positional 2 to install-diffract-tools.sh and advertise-diffract-tools.sh). So diffract-tool-add.sh docker-cps the CURRENT install+advertise scripts (found next to the registry = NemoClaw/agents/hermes/, or the repo path) into the sandbox /tmp and runs THOSE, not the baked ones; then docker exec install --only TOOL (git clone + build, uses the container bridge net), advertise --only TOOL, and rm the skills snapshot. VERIFIED end-to-end on the live box by running the wrapper for ghl (idempotent reinstall): cloned gohighlevel-cli, tsc build, symlink, SKILL.md, ===DONE rc=0===, and ghl still returns 25 contacts after. Secrets are NOT part of add - the user connects via the existing POST /api/tools afterward. Registry is written on the box (repo copy) so it also re-bakes on next recreate; for durability across a fresh git redeploy the registry change should be committed. Deployed: pull + npm run build + systemctl restart diffractui.

Symptom: /chat or /agent/ returns HTTP 502 while the Diffract UI shows the forward as active. Root cause: an openshell forward (ssh -L 127.0.0.1:9119:127.0.0.1:9119 via openshell ssh-proxy) was created for port 9119 - e.g. by a manual restart-the-forward or nemoclaw recover. An openshell forward lands in the sandbox WORKLOAD netns, but the agent chat dashboard (hermes dashboard --tui, listening 0.0.0.0:9119 inside the container) lives in the CONTAINER netns, so the tunnel forwards to nothing -> 502/000 (the UI still says active because the ssh process is alive). The two ports use DIFFERENT transports by design (see setup.sh sandbox-port-forwarder.sh): 9119 = host socat 127.0.0.1:9119 -> CONTAINER_IP:9118 -> in-container socat -> 127.0.0.1:9119 (loopback re-origination so the dashboard sees a loopback peer for its WS anti-rebinding check); 8642 = openshell forward (ssh -L) because the OpenAI-compat gateway IS in the workload netns. Diagnose: ss -ltnp | grep :9119 shows ssh instead of socat; openshell forward list shows a 9119 entry. The in-container dashboard + the :9118 hop being up confirms the only broken link is the host listener.
resolution: Stop the wrong forward then let the socat hop rebuild: openshell forward stop 9119 hermes ; systemctl restart sandbox-port-forwarder.service. Verify host curl http://127.0.0.1:9119/agent/ = 200 and https://srv1670849.hstgr.cloud/agent/ = 200, and that the 9119 listener is socat (not ssh). Do NOT use openshell forward for 9119; only 8642 uses openshell forward.

description: Symptom: /chat and /agent/ return HTTP 502 (recurring, came back on every dashboard load) while the Diffract UI reports the forward active. ROOT CAUSE: diffractui /api/forward POST ran openshell forward start PORT SANDBOX --background AND first killed (lsof + process.kill) whatever held the port; Dashboard.tsx calls fetch(/api/forward?sandbox=NAME&port=9119) on mount. So every UI load destroyed the correct host socat hop on 9119 and replaced it with an openshell forward (ssh -L via openshell ssh-proxy) that lands in the sandbox WORKLOAD netns - but the agent chat dashboard (hermes dashboard --tui, 0.0.0.0:9119) lives in the CONTAINER netns, so the tunnel forwarded to nothing -> 502/000 (UI shows active because the ssh process is alive). The two ports use DIFFERENT transports by design (setup.sh sandbox-port-forwarder.sh): 9119 = host socat 127.0.0.1:9119 -> CONTAINER_IP:9118 -> in-container socat -> 127.0.0.1:9119 (loopback re-origination for the dashboard WS anti-rebinding peer check); 8642 = openshell forward (its OpenAI-compat gateway IS in the workload netns). Diagnose: ss -ltnp grep :9119 shows ssh instead of socat; openshell forward list shows a 9119 entry; the in-container dashboard + :9118 hop are healthy so the only broken link is the host listener. Also note /api/forward (and the other /api control routes) are behind the Phase-0 admin auth gate, so only the logged-in UI reaches it.
resolution: PERMANENT FIX (commit e908575): diffractui/src/app/api/forward/route.ts now treats 9119/9118 as service-managed (socat). For those ports POST never kills the listener and never runs openshell forward; it health-checks http://127.0.0.1:9119/agent/ and only self-heals if down (openshell forward stop 9119 to clear any stray, then systemctl restart sandbox-port-forwarder.service). GET reports real reachability instead of calling a dead tunnel active. Other ports keep openshell forward. Manual recovery if ever needed: openshell forward stop 9119 hermes ; systemctl restart sandbox-port-forwarder.service ; verify ss -ltnp grep :9119 shows socat and /agent/ = 200. Never openshell-forward 9119; only 8642 uses openshell forward.

name: gateway-watchdog-chat-backend-selfheal
description: Added scripts/diffract-gateway-watchdog.sh + diffract-gateway-watchdog.service (installed/enabled by setup.sh, so every deployment self-heals). It closes the gap that left chat 502 for hours: the sandbox-port-forwarder keepalive only health-checks 9119 (dashboard) and only rebuilds a forward when the port is FREE - it does NOT recover a dead-but-listening 8642 tunnel or a crashed gateway daemon. The watchdog curl-health-checks the chat backend http://127.0.0.1:8642/v1/models every 20s and, after a 2-tick debounce, recovers least-disruptive-first: if the gateway daemon is up (docker exec pgrep gateway run) it restarts sandbox-port-forwarder (whose cleanup_fwd kills the stale ssh -L then rebuilds the forward); if the daemon is DOWN it docker-restarts the sandbox container to relaunch it, then rebuilds forwards. Capped at MAX_RECOVERIES=4 then it ALERTS (log + optional DIFFRACT_WATCHDOG_ALERT_URL webhook) instead of thrashing or auto-recreating (destructive). Tunables via env. VERIFIED: service active+enabled, idle (no actions) while healthy. NOTE from testing: stopping the 8642 forward outright is recovered by the forwarder keepalive in ~10s (free port -> ensure_8642_forward rebuilds) BEFORE the watchdog's 40s debounce - so that case is double-covered; the watchdog's unique value is the daemon-down + dead-but-listening cases the forwarder misses, plus the alert-within-2min behavior (vs silent hours). Did NOT live-kill the gateway to prove the daemon-down path because it risks re-wedging a freshly-recovered chat; both recovery actions are individually proven (manual forwarder restart rebuilds 8642; docker restart relaunches the gateway when not wedged).

name: openshell-0057-inference-through-netns-proven
description: CRITICAL de-risk PROVEN in the isolated dind lab: on OpenShell 0.0.57, inference routes correctly from a workload in the SEALED network namespace - the scariest upgrade unknown (does the netns break the agent's ability to reach the LLM). Test: in the lab 0.0.57 stack, created an nvidia-type provider (dummy key), attached it to a 0.0.57 sandbox (t2), then openshell inference set --system --provider nv --model meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct --no-verify (the --system route is the one the agent harness uses; the user-facing route alone is NOT enough - inference.local checks the system route). Then from inside t2 via openshell sandbox exec, curl -k https://inference.local/v1/models returned HTTP 200 with the full NVIDIA NIM model catalog (real upstream response routed sandbox -> netns -> OpenShell inference router -> NVIDIA). Before configuring it, inference.local returned a STRUCTURED OpenShell error (cluster inference is not configured) not a network failure - proving the netns reaches the router; the 503 was missing backend config, not a routing block. Also confirmed in the same session: egress enforcement works (non-allowed example.com -> CONNECT 403 denied). The inference config command is openshell inference set (both 0.0.39 and 0.0.57 have it; the binary has no cluster subcommand - the hint text is misleading), so nemoclaw onboard configures inference the SAME way on both versions. NET: the 0.0.57 OpenShell foundation is proven for all agent fundamentals - inference (think), credential injection to a daemon (tools in chat), gateway-JWT auth, and egress enforcement. Remaining risk is the NemoClaw onboard integration/config, not the OpenShell mechanics.

ufw default-deny INPUT silently blocks the internal container to host:8080 gRPC the OpenShell sandbox supervisor uses to fetch its policy, so the sandbox never reaches Ready and the gateway never launches. It surfaces only as downstream symptoms: chat 502s, gateway crash-loops, and Something went wrong setting up your sandbox recreate failures, never pointing at the firewall. The 2026-06-09 outage was this: ufw activated on a reboot with only 22/80/443 allowed.
resolution: Add an interface-agnostic rule ufw allow from 172.16.0.0/12 to any port 8080 proto tcp. Subnet match survives docker bridge renames like br-hash where allow in on iface would not. ufw allow persists even when ufw is inactive, so it also guards a later ufw enable on reboot. Shipped as scripts/diffract-ensure-gateway-firewall.sh, invoked by setup.sh at install and by the gateway watchdog as its first recovery step since a container restart alone cannot fix a firewall block. Does NOT cover the Hostinger edge firewall, a separate cloud-panel layer.

name: universal-tool-in-chat-registry-driven
description: General solution so ANY connected CLI tool is usable by the chat agent with zero per-tool code. A tool is connected when a registry entry in diffract-tools.json has a matching OpenShell provider. scripts/diffract-tool-sync.sh computes from registry intersect providers: providers mode prints the comma list for NEMOCLAW_SANDBOX_EXTRA_PROVIDERS (the onboard attaches them at sandbox create), egress mode applies each tool apiHosts and binaries from the registry. The diffractui deploy route calls providers before onboard and egress after, so every deploy or recreate wires all connected tools for chat. Adding a new tool is just a registry entry plus connect, no code. The onboard NEMOCLAW_SANDBOX_EXTRA_PROVIDERS support was added in NemoClaw onboard.ts. Live 0.0.57 cutover proved it end to end: chat agent ran ghl contacts list and returned real contacts.

title: Connected-tool set is gateway-independent for create-time attach
rationale: Deploy route computes attach-at-create provider list before the gateway starts; a gateway-independent persistent record avoids silently dropping tools on a recovery deploy. Verified end-to-end 2026-06-10.

description: The ghl CLI search returned the unfiltered default list because it sent the GHL API search param as q; GHL rejects that with 422 property q should not exist and expects query. The CLI was inconsistent: media command sent query correctly, but contacts, conversations, opportunities, and surveys sent q. A 422 means the request authenticated and reached GHL, so it was a CLI request-shape bug, not a Diffract or OpenShell or credential issue. The correct managed exec path to run a baked tool against the live API is openshell sandbox exec, not raw docker exec which lacks the proxy and credential.
resolution: Fixed via the diffract-tools.json install patch hook (the designed source-fixup layer alongside the axios-to-fetch patch): a sed rewriting params.q = opts.query to params.query across src/commands before build. Verified live by live-patching the running sandbox dist, after which contacts list with an email or phone returned the exact match. Durability: the install patch only bakes in at hermes IMAGE REBUILD, not on a plain sandbox recreate which reuses the image; the live-patched dist is ephemeral because diffract-persist excludes the tools dir. Committed a82f552.

name: agent-dashboard-url-and-theme
description: The deployed agent dashboard (hermes/web) is exposed publicly at Caddy path /agent/ (handle_path /agent/* + /assets/* -> 127.0.0.1:9119); / -> diffractui:3000; /v1/* -> chat 8642. Its background is driven by hermes/web src/index.css :root + src/themes/presets.ts defaultTheme; BUILTIN_THEMES[name] wins over any backend-shipped theme def, so the frontend presets are authoritative. Default theme recolored teal #041c1c -> solid dark maroon #4a0e16 (warm-glow #96243447) and relabeled Diffract Maroon. Deploy = build web_dist on VPS host then docker cp web_dist/. into sandbox container :/opt/hermes/web_dist (web-only overlay, no restart). Note: served index.html still contains window.__HERMES_DASHBOARD_EMBEDDED_CHAT__ injected by in-container web_server.py (functional flag, not visible, do not patch live).

name: hermes-image-build-and-baked-tool-changes
description: Two builds exist. (1) Dockerfile.base -> base image tagged by git sha (nemoclaw-hermes-sandbox-base-local:<sha>): node, apt deps, hermes agent venv. Rebuilt only on forceBaseImageRebuild or sha change. (2) The per-create runtime Dockerfile -> openshell/sandbox-from:<timestamp>, built FROM the base on EVERY sandbox create; THIS is where diffract tools install: step 13 COPY agents/hermes/diffract-tools.json, step 15 RUN install-diffract-tools.sh (git clone each tool repo at ref, apply the registry patch field, npm build), and advertise. CORRECTION to earlier belief: a baked TOOL fix does NOT require a forced base rebuild. Because install runs in the per-create build, a tool fix (registry patch in diffract-tools.json, or an upstream tool-repo fix) propagates on the next recreate (dashboard deploy OR nemoclaw rebuild) once the build-context registry changes (Docker cache on step 13 COPY invalidates step 15 -> re-clone) or upstream changed. The build-context registry the Dockerfile COPYs is /root/diffract-main/NemoClaw/agents/hermes/diffract-tools.json (the repo copy), NOT the staged /usr/local/share/diffract copy used by host connect/sync. nemoclaw <name> rebuild is the SAFE destructive path (build base FIRST so a build failure is non-destructive, then backup, destroy, recreate via onboard --resume, restore); its recreate honors NEMOCLAW_SANDBOX_EXTRA_PROVIDERS (set it + source NVIDIA_API_KEY from /etc/diffractui.env) and does NOT re-apply diffract tool egress (run diffract-tool-sync.sh egress <sandbox> after). Verified 2026-06-10: rebuild baked params.query into the dist, procs-with-GHL=9, ghl-in-chat live.

Agent dashboard branding (e.g. solid-maroon bg, removing the @nous-research/ui filler-bg0.webp Greek-figure background image in hermes/web/src/components/Backdrop.tsx) reverts on every sandbox recreate, because the dashboard web_dist is NOT baked into the sandbox image (NemoClaw/agents/hermes/Dockerfile has no web build) and a live docker-cp overlay dies with the container.
resolution: The persistence-critical source is /usr/local/lib/hermes-agent/hermes_cli/web_dist/ — sandbox-port-forwarder.sh docker-cp's THAT into /opt/hermes/web_dist on every sandbox start (overlay, not wipe). To make a dashboard rebrand survive recreate you must rebuild the HOST install: cp changed hermes/web/src files (+ index.html for the title) into /usr/local/lib/hermes-agent/web/ then 'cd /usr/local/lib/hermes-agent/web && npm run build'. Updating only the repo (/root/diffract-main) or the running container is NOT enough. Note the port-forwarder overlays so the orphan filler webp may linger unreferenced (harmless; index.html/CSS/Backdrop no longer reference it). Served at Caddy /agent/.

description: nemoclaw <name> rebuild force-rebuilds the base image and recreates the sandbox, but its recreate goes through onboard --resume, which SKIPS the agent_setup and inference steps (logs show [resume] Skipping inference and [resume] Skipping agent_setup). On a FRESH daemon that skip is fatal: the agent config.yaml is left with providers: {} empty (root:root 640), so the Hermes agent returns HTTP 500 No inference provider configured even though the gateway inference route is healthy. nemoclaw inference set fixes the gateway route but its sandbox-config sync fails with Cannot read hermes config / Is the sandbox running (the sync cannot read the root-owned config), so it does NOT repopulate the agent config. So nemoclaw rebuild is UNSAFE for baking an image change on this setup: it breaks inference. Net: the destructive rebuild was unnecessary too, because install-diffract-tools runs in the per-create sandbox-from build, so updating the build-context registry plus a normal dashboard deploy would have baked the tool fix AND run agent_setup.
resolution: Recover with a FULL, fresh onboard (never --resume): the dashboard destroy+deploy flow, which runs agent_setup, attaches ghl via NEMOCLAW_SANDBOX_EXTRA_PROVIDERS from the connected-tools record, applies egress, and reuses the now-fixed base image. To bake a baked-tool fix in future, just update the build-context registry (or upstream repo) and do a normal dashboard deploy — do NOT use nemoclaw rebuild.
